Kunst en Antiek - Timed Online Deze veiling omvat een ruime selectie aan antiek en 19e en 20e-eeuwse keramiek, met een rijk aanbod van vazen, borden, schalen, kannen, figuren en beelden, schotels, kopjes, potten, tegels en meer. Het betreft producten van onder andere de fabrieken van P. Regout & Co. / De Sphinx (1834–1969), Société Céramique (1863–1958) en Mosa (1883–1996).
Chinese Hardhouten Plantenstandaard met Marmeren Inlegblad Chinese Hardwood Plant Stand with Marble Inset Top, Late 19th Century. China, circa 1880 to 1900. H. 76 cm.

Anglo-Indische gesneden teak houten tafel. Anglo-Indische gesneden teak houten tafel. India, circa 1900. Vierkante tafel vervaardigd uit massief teak, met een uitzonderlijk fijn gesneden blad voorzien van florale en geometrische ornamentiek, rustend op vier rijk uitgewerkte poten in de vorm van vogelklauwen met bal. 62 x 88.5 x 88 cm. Anglo-Indian carved teak wood table. India, circa 1900. Square table with very finely detailed carved table top, standing on four bird claw and ball feet. Anglo-Indische meubelstukken zoals deze tafel werden vervaardigd in de voormalige Britse koloniën in India en combineren de verfijnde handwerktechnieken van Indiase houtsnijders met Westerse vormen en stijlen die populair waren in Victoriaans Engeland. Dergelijke meubels waren geliefd bij Britse kolonisten en reizigers rond 1900 en worden tegenwoordig gewaardeerd om hun ambachtelijke kwaliteit en decoratieve rijkdom.
Peter GHYCZY (1940-2022). Jaren 70 glazen salontafel met bronzen frame en poten, ca. 1970. 122 x 77 x 42 cm. 1970's bronze and glass coffee table.

Massief houten salontafel met opbergruimte Europa 20e eeuw. Rechthoekige salontafel vervaardigd uit massief hout, met opklapbaar blad voorzien van metalen kettingen en scharnieren. Het interieur biedt ruime opbergruimte. De tafel is opgebouwd uit vlakke panelen met rechte poten en messing handgrepen aan de zijkanten, uitgevoerd in warme, donkerbruine houttint met een verzorgde afwerking. 140 x 46 x 80 cm. Solid wood coffee table with storage space.

Louis-Philippe Stoel Louis-Philippe stoel. Frankrijk, circa 1840–1860. Mahoniehouten stoel met gebogen rugleuning en licht gebogen poten, kenmerkend voor de Louis-Philippe-stijl. De rug is sierlijk gevormd met een gesloten rugspijl en een uitgesneden middenstuk, terwijl de zitting is bekleed met een bloemrijk gobelinweefsel, afgewerkt met koperen sierspijkers. Louis-Philippe chair with embroidered seat.

Perzisch tapijt handgeknoopt - Groot formaat Perzië (Iran), circa 1900. Handgeknoopt wollen tapijt met een dicht patroon van florale ranken en rozetten op een rood veld, omlijst door een meerkleurige rand met bloemmotieven op een donkerblauwe fond. De verfijnde detaillering en het subtiele kleurenspel zijn kenmerkend voor de hoogwaardige Perzische weefkunst uit het begin van de 20e eeuw. 310 x 250 cm. Persian carpet hand knotted - Large size.

Uitklapbare kaarttafel ca. 1900 Uitklapbare kaarttafel. Europa, circa 1900. Vierkante opklapbare kaarttafel in notenhout, met geprofileerde rand en fijn gesneden details langs het tafelblad en de poten. Het blad is voorzien van een scharnierend middenpaneel dat geopend kan worden voor het kaarspel en is bekleed met een ingelegd weefselpatroon. De tafel rust op vier getailleerde, gecanneleerde poten. 107 x 92 x 70.5 cm. A Folding Card table ca 1900. With beautifully inlaid wood and fabric upholstery.
2 Antieke fauteuils in Louis XV-stijl 2 Antieke fauteuils in Louis XV-stijl met geborduurde bekleding. 2 Antique armchairs Louis XV style with embroidered upholstery. Sold for €40.
2 Houten Boho stoelen Houtsnijwerk Wabi Sabi met geborduurde bekleding. 2 Wooden Boho Chairs Woodcarving Wabi Sabi with embroidered upholstery.

Schwarzwäld gesneden houten sieraden doos 19e eeuw Duitsland, Schwarzwäld-regio, einde 19e eeuw. Rechthoekige doos met bloem- en geometrische motieven, het deksel met twee nestelende patrijzen, met slot en sleutel. Schwarzwälder Schatulle. Deutschland, Schwarzwäldregion, späten 19. Jahrhundert. Rechteckige Dose mit floralen und geometrischen Motiven, der Deckel mit zwei nistenden Rebhühnern, mit Schloss und Schlüssel. 20 x 22.5 x 13.5 cm. Black Forest Carved wooden jewellery Box. Germany, Black Forest Region, late 19th century. Rectangular box with floral and geometric motifs, the cover with two nesting grouses, with lock and key.

Groot eiken kruisbeeld met gesneden hardhout Corpus Christi. Eikenhouten kruis met gesneden corpus Christi in expressionistische stijl. Wandkruis uit eikenhout met een gesneden Christusfiguur in gestileerde expressionistische vormentaal, weergegeven met uitgestrekte armen, licht gebogen hoofd en sterk gemodelleerde, magere romp. De lendendoek is vereenvoudigd tot hoekige plooien, het gelaat langgerekt en diep uitgesneden. Het kruis bestaat uit twee rechthoekige eikenhouten balken, waarbij de armen van Christus iets buiten de dwarsbalk uitsteken. 80 x 63 cm. Wall-mounted oak crucifix with a carved figure of Christ in stylised expressionist manner, shown with outstretched arms, gently bowed head, and emaciated torso. The loincloth is simplified into angular folds, and the facial features are elongated and deeply carved. The cross is constructed from two intersecting rectangular oak beams, with the arms of Christ extending slightly beyond the crossbar.

Le Cercle et le Bilboquet, naar Jacques Stella (1596-1657). Handgekleurde gravure op papier. Onder de voorstelling voorzien van de titel “Le Cercle et le Bilboquet” en een gedicht in twee strofen. Linksonder gesigneerd “Inventé par J. Stella”, rechtsonder “Gravé par C. Bonaventure Stella”, genummerd “Pl. 39”. 16 x 19 cm. Le Cercle et le Bilboquet, after Jacques Stella (1596-1657). Hand-colored engraving on paper. With title “Le Cercle et le Bilboquet” and a two-stanza poem. Signed lower left "Inventé par J. Stella", lower right "Gravé par C. Bonaventure Stella", numbered "Pl. 39".

Alphons VOLDERS (1899-1993). ‘Landschap met hoeve en bomen’. Houtskool op papier. Linksonder gesigneerd en gedateerd "A. Volders 1935". 64 x 49 cm. Alphons VOLDERS (1899-1993) ‘Landscape with Farm and Trees’. Charcoal on paper. Signed and dated lower left “A. Volders 1935”.
Anglo-Indiaas Houten Plantenstandaard, Eind 19e Eeuw India, circa 1880 tot 1900. H. 92 cm. Anglo-Indian Plant Stand, Late 19th century. India, circa 1880-1900. Sold for €25.
Chinees Gelakt Houten Plantenstandaard China, circa 1900. H. 76 cm. Chinese Lacquered Wood Plant Stand. China, circa 1900.

Indiase Gesneden Houten Bijzettafel met Marmeren Blad, Begin 20e Eeuw India, circa 1900 tot 1920. 50 x 53 x 53 cm. Indian Carved Wooden Side Table with Marble Top, Early 20th Century. India, circa 1900 to 1920.

Vier Houten Etagère Tafels Four Decorative Wooden Stands, 20th Century. Sold for €85. Auction: Kunst en Antiek - Timed Online, ending 15th Jan, 2026. This auction includes a wide selection of antique and 19th and 20th-century ceramics, with a rich offer of vases, plates, bowls, cups, figures, and more. The products are from factories such as P. Regout & Co. / De Sphinx (1834–1969), Société Céramique (1863–1958), and Mosa (1883–1996).
Zes diverse Chinese plantentafels Six Chinese Plant Stands. Ca. 1900. H. 82 cm. en kleiner.
Set van zes houten armstoelen met spijlenrug 20e eeuw. Zes armstoelen van hout met afgeronde arm- en rugpartij en verticale spijlen, op gebogen poten. Losse, lichtgekleurde zitkussens.

John Goodison (English/American, 1834-1892) Oil on Canvas 1853, 'Bird Hunting, Kaaterskill Falls', H 55.5" W 32" Frame Size: H 60" W 36" Signed lower right and dated "Detroit 1853". Depicting a hunter shooting game birds at Kaaterskill Falls, New York. Provenance: From the collection of Maxine Pflanzer, Minden City, MI. By descent to her children. Lot Essay: John Goodison emigrated to Detroit to open a school of drawing in 1852 and this work was painted in the following year. While there is no documented evidence of his having traveled to upstate New York, this work is clearly shows the influence that the Hudson River School. In the 1850's Kaaterskill Falls was the most famous and reproduced waterfall in American art. Therefore, it is not out of the realm of possibility that the artist either traveled to sketch the falls upon landing in America or after having established himself in Detroit, and return to his studio int to complete larger works. Written by: Walker, Joe, Dimensions: H 55.5" W 32" Frame Size: H 60" W 36". Having museum quality conservation which included a new stretcher and lining. Craquelure has been expertly stabilized. Annual Thanksgiving Auction 2025, Day 2, 11/30/25 Kaminski Auctions has been commissioned to sell the estate of David Martin, the world-renowned petroleum geologist. The sale also features a remarkable selection of vintage jewelry from a Miami Beach collection, a vintage wine collection from an old New England family, items from a West Palm Beach collection, a Sargent Road Brookline, Massachusetts family fine art collection from West Palm Beach, and 1968 and 1977 Gibson Les Paul guitars, both signed by Les Paul. Additionally, the auction includes an important family collection of Chinese art, scrolls, and porcelains from the Zhang Dinghuang (1895-1986) Family, of Peking and Tokyo. Other highlights include seven important Ming Dynasty scrolls by Wen Po-jen, which were sold at Sotheby Parke Bernet, New York, in 1984. Auction to be held Saturday and Sunday, Nov. 29th and 30th, at the Kaminski Gallery, 117 Elliott St., Beverly, MA Preview Nov. 23rd-30th, 10AM-5PM each day, except Thanksgiving Day (closed).

Session 2 - Chinese Jade, Ceramics, Snuff Bottles and Works of Art 中國古代玉器、瓷器、鼻煙壺及藝術品（二） This session forms part of Spring Welcome Selections | January 2026 Chinese Art and Antiques Auction and features a curated selection of Chinese jade, ceramics, snuff bottles, and works of art. The jade offerings include pendants, plaques, fittings, and small carvings in a variety of materials. Ceramic works comprise functional and decorative wares representing traditional forms and glazing techniques. A dedicated group of snuff bottles includes examples in glass, hardstone, and other materials. Complementing these are additional works of art such as wood carvings, lacquerware, metal objects, and scholar’s items, together presenting a concise overview of Chinese craftsmanship.

Session 1 - Chinese Jade, Ceramics and Works of Art - No Reserve 中國古代玉器、瓷器及藝術品無保留價專場（一） This no-reserve session presents a broad and dynamic offering of Chinese jade, ceramics, and works of art. The selection includes carved jade pendants, plaques, and ritual-inspired forms spanning a range of styles and periods. Ceramic highlights encompass monochrome-glazed wares, celadon pieces, and enameled porcelains that reflect the technical refinement of various kilns. Complementing the jades and ceramics are scholar’s objects and decorative works in bronze, lacquer, and hardstone. United by craftsmanship and material diversity, the session offers collectors opportunities across categories, from utilitarian wares to finely carved objects of display.

Session 2 - Chinese Jade, Ceramics, Snuff Bottles, Textiles and Works of Art 中國古代玉器、瓷器、鼻煙壺、織繡及藝術品（二） Session Two offers an expanded and richly varied selection of Chinese jades, ceramics, snuff bottles, textiles, and works of art. Carved jades range from pendants, plaques, and rings to decorative carvings that emphasize material, polish, and form. Ceramics include vessels in monochrome and enamel decoration, alongside scholar’s wares and decorative objects. A substantial group of snuff bottles—crafted in porcelain, glass, and hardstone—highlights miniature artistry and refined finishing. The session is further distinguished by lacquer and hardwood works, including multi-panel inlaid folding screens, hanging panels, and finely made furniture such as tables, stands, cabinets, and chairs, showcasing techniques of hardstone inlay, mother-of-pearl embellishment, and painted decoration. Embroidered and woven textiles add another dimension, underscoring the breadth of traditional craftsmanship across materials and formats.

Session 3 - Chinese Painting and Calligraphy 中國書畫（三） The third session is primarily devoted to Chinese painting and calligraphy, presenting landscapes, bird-and-flower subjects, figure compositions, and works in a range of script styles. The sale opens with refined landscapes associated with Pu Ru, reflecting the elegance and literati sensibility that shaped twentieth-century ink tradition. Calligraphic works by Yu Youren demonstrate the expressive strength of modern cursive script, while Lot 475, a floral hanging scroll by Zhang Daqian, stands as a notable highlight, exemplifying his assured brushwork and vibrant compositional balance. Spanning hanging scrolls, handscrolls, album leaves, and fan formats, the session illustrates the enduring vitality of ink culture across generations. Complementing the Chinese works are a small number of paintings and prints from other East Asian traditions, offering broader cultural context within the field of Asian art.

COLLECTIF, Complete "Connaissance de l'orgue" magazine, issu - Lot 4 COLLECTIF, Complete "Connaissance de l'orgue" magazine, issues 1 to 92 In-8, paperback - Good condition. The set we're presenting on February 19 is exceptional, not for its purely bibliophilic qualities - although... - but exceptional because it comprises a collection of numerous books and magazines devoted exclusively to the world of organs from France and around the world. And organs in good condition, restored or in the process of being restored, are to be found almost everywhere on every continent. Patiently assembled over the course of a lifetime by Professor B..., this collection constitutes a first-rate documentary base on this instrument present in our churches as well as our cathedrals. Among the nuggets in this library is L'art du facteur d'orgues by Dom François Bédos de Celles, the Holy Grail of all organ books, here in its Original Edition published between 1776 and 1778, complete with 137 plates in folio format. The catalog includes almost thirty lots, offering either individual books, or sets of books or magazines, many of them in old editions now sold out by new booksellers. And for those who are bilingual, trilingual or more, books and booklets in German, English and Italian.

A PAINTED PINE HOUSEKEEPERS CUPBOARD 19th century, the moulded cornice above two doors before two internal shelves, the lower super-structure with central bank of three drawers flanked by a drawer either side and a panelled cupboard, 205cm high x 200cm wide x 52cm deep. This lot is in good overall condition with scratching and staining throughout. There is evidence of old worm which has resulted in the use of something like creosote. This can be detected by smell but is no evident on the interior or exterior. The old worming has resulted in small losses to the timbers in corners and edges throughout, which is superficial and largely aesthetic and has not weakened the cupboard. The plinth timbers below are lacking on the right return angle and have exposed new timbers for the base. There is staining to the shelving and the drawers which have old newspaper linings. The cupboard is stable and fit for purpose with smooth running/opening drawers and doors. This does not separate for transportation.

Sans titre Peintre et poète français, Atlan est une figure majeure de l’abstraction lyrique de l’après-guerre. Né à Constantine dans une famille juive berbère, il s’installe à Paris dans les années 1930 où il fréquente les milieux intellectuels et artistiques. Résistant pendant la Seconde Guerre mondiale, son œuvre porte durablement la trace d’une quête spirituelle et symbolique profonde. Atlan développe un langage pictural très personnel, fait de formes organiques, de signes archaïsants et de couleurs intenses, à mi-chemin entre abstraction et mythologie. Son travail se rapproche de celui d’artistes avec lesquels il entretient des affinités esthétiques et intellectuelles, tels que Jean Dubuffet, Hans Hartung, Pierre Soulages ou encore Nicolas de Staël. Proche de l’esprit du groupe CoBrA sans lui appartenir formellement, Atlan occupe une place singulière dans l’art moderne français, entre primitivisme, lyrisme et pensée philosophique.

Onion, from Campbell's Soup I, 1968 Sérigraphie sur papier signé et numéroté 173/250 au dos. Publié par Factory Additions, New York, imprimeur Salvatore Silkscreen Co., Inc., New York. H. 35 in, L. 23 in, P. 0 in. Diameter: 0 in. Figure centrale du Pop Art, Andy Warhol a profondément transformé le rapport entre art, image et culture de masse. Aux côtés de Roy Lichtenstein, Claes Oldenburg ou James Rosenquist, il impose dans les années 1960 un nouveau langage visuel fondé sur l’appropriation, la répétition et l’iconisation des objets du quotidien. La série des Campbell’s Soup, initiée dès 1962, constitue le socle historique de son œuvre et demeure son motif le plus emblématique sur le marché. Réalisée en 1968, Campbell’s Soup I : Onion appartient au premier portfolio de sérigraphies consacré à ce sujet. Par sa frontalité, son format imposant et sa lisibilité immédiate, cette planche érige un produit industriel en icône absolue, comparable, dans l’imaginaire collectif, aux portraits de Marilyn ou aux Brillo Boxes. Encadré, restaurations.

Composition, 1967 Figure majeure de l’abstraction française d’après-guerre, André Marfaing naît à Toulouse en 1925 et s’impose dès les années 1950 par une recherche picturale exigeante, centrée sur l’essentiel. Son œuvre, développée autour de la confrontation du noir et du blanc, explore avec rigueur les rapports de masse, de rythme et de lumière. Son travail peut être rapproché de celui de Pierre Soulages par l’usage structurant du noir, tout en affirmant une écriture plus intériorisée et architecturée. Sa démarche dialogue également avec celle de Hans Hartung, avec laquelle elle partage une attention constante à la construction du champ pictural.

Portrait de violoniste, 1981 Huile sur panneau signé et daté en bas à droite. Peintre figuratif finlandais reconnu dans son pays, Veikko Marttinen développe une oeuvre centrée sur le portrait, caractérisée par une forte intensité psychologique et une grande sobriété formelle. La frontalité de la figure, la stylisation mesurée des traits et l’attention portée au regard inscrivent sa peinture dans une figuration moderne européenne pouvant évoquer Amedeo Modigliani, tandis que la retenue expressive et la présence silencieuse du modèle rappellent certains portraits de Balthus. Bénéficiant d’une reconnaissance institutionnelle solide en Finlande, notamment avec l’attribution du prix Pro Finlandia, c'est un artiste rare sur le marché français.

La Sortie du pesage à Auteuil Huile sur toile signée en bas à droite, titrée et contresignée au dos. Formée dans un cadre académique, elle expose dès le début du XXe siècle au Salon des Artistes Français, où elle est régulièrement récompensée, obtenant notamment le prix Rosa-Bonheur, distinction emblématique de son talent pour la représentation du monde animal. Son œuvre se distingue par de nombreuses scènes hippiques — courses, paddocks, marchés aux chevaux — dans lesquelles elle saisit avec une grande justesse le mouvement, la tension et la dimension sociale des hippodromes. À partir des années 1920, ses voyages en Afrique du Nord (Maroc, Tunisie) enrichissent sa peinture d’une lumière nouvelle et d’un regard attentif aux scènes de la vie quotidienne, prolongeant son intérêt pour le réel dans un registre orientaliste mesuré. Son travail connaît aujourd’hui une redécouverte, illustrée par l’exposition au Musée Auguste Chabaud du 6 février au 7 mai 2026 "Adrienne Jouclard, figure marquante du Montparnasse des peintres" qui met en lumière la cohérence et la diversité de son parcours au sein de la peinture figurative française du XXe siècle.

John HOPPNER (1758-1810), - Lot 17 John HOPPNER (1758-1810), R.A. Portrait of Lady James Gordon (1783-) née Mary Lucy Elizabeth Glendonwyn. Oil on canvas 130 × 94 cm. Provenance: - Probably commissioned from the Gordon family - Walmer House, Kent, seat of the Lord Warden of the Cinque Ports, late 19th century, - Edward Brandus (1857-1937), art dealer, New-York, - his sale, New-York, 1914, lot 99 - Probably acquired at this sale by Edward Julius Berwind (1848-1936), industrialist - Berwind Collection, The Elms, Newport, Rhode Island, - Then, by inheritance, to Edith Baroness von Kleist (1879-1963), née Berwind, niece of the Berwind, niece of the former, Domaine du Saugy, Genthod, canton of Geneva, - then, by descent, to the present day. Bibliography: Collection of paintings by the ancient and modern masters, to be sold at public sale by order of Mr. Edward Brandus [March 12th and 13th, 1914] Sale under the management of the Fifth avenue art galleries, New York, p.99. The model's husband, Sir James Gordon (1779-1843), was "8th Baronet of Letterfourie, 7th Baronet of Gordonstoun", from a prestigious Scottish family. The title of Baronet Gordon de Gordonstoun was created on May 28, 1625 in the Barony of Nova Scotia for Robert Gordon de Gordonstoun, fourth son of Alexander Gordon, 12th Earl of Sutherland (?-15). Sutherland (?-1594). Until 1908, this was the most prestigious baronetcy in Scotland. Our portrait, which was part of the selection of the French dealer Edward Brandus in the United States, was acquired by Edward Julius Berwind. The Berwind family was a dynasty of American industrialists of German origin, who left their mark on the economic history of the United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Based in Pennsylvania, brothers Edward and Charles Berwind founded the Berwind-White Coal Mining Company, which became one of the country's largest privately-owned coal mining companies. Their industrial success made them influential figures of the "Gilded Age", exemplifying the great captains of industry closely linked to the development of railways, steel and the merchant marine. The family's accumulated wealth enabled them to build sumptuous residences, such as The Elms in Newport (Rhode Island), a symbol of their social ascendancy and their proximity to the elite of the era, where our painting was exhibited.

Successeur d'Alexis Renaud, 16 quai Pelletier, Hippolyte Puche fut reçu maître le 10 novembre 1847 à Paris. Il se consacra comme lui aux productions religieuses, dont il devint un maître, à une période clé pour la reconstitution par l'Église de son patrimoine d'orfèvrerie, fortement atteint durant la Révolution. On trouve notamment un calice similaire par son décor en l'église Saint-Pierre de Dangé, à Dangé-Saint-Romain (inv. n°IM86005980, reproduit en ligne).

Boîte en argent, de forme ovale, le corps repoussé d'entrelacs fleuris en frise tournante, le couvercle orné d'un portrait repoussé en bas-relief du philosophe Théophraste, entouré d'une tresse de laurier à la manière d'un médaillon, et bordure ondulée. Lettres "AELFVZ" gravées sous la base. Boîte en argent, de forme ovale, le corps repoussé d'entrelacs fleuris en frise tournante, le couvercle orné d'un portrait repoussé en bas-relief du philosophe Théophraste, entouré d'une tresse de laurier à la manière d'un médaillon, et bordure ondulée. Lettres "AELFVZ" gravées sous la base. Maître-orfèvre : Adolf Gaap, reçu maître-orfèvre à Augsbourg en 1664, il est actif jusqu'en 1695. Un certain nombre de ses œuvres sont conservées, notamment des gobelets couverts (musée juif de Prague, musée princier Hohenzollern, Sigmaringen, Musée d'État du Wurtemberg, Stuttgart). Augsbourg, probablement 1688-1989 (Seling n°0940) ou 1691-1695 (Seling n°1010) H. : 6,5 - L. : 10 - P. : 11 cm Poids : 172,2 g (Usures, petits enfoncements). On retrouve une boîte de toilette identiquement décrite et de mêmes dimensions, par Adolf Gaap, mais ornée du portrait du philosophe grec Carnéade sur le couvercle, vente Christie's Genève du 30 septembre 1996, lot n°211. Estimation CHF 1,200 - CHF 1,800 ; résultat : CHF 1,265. Théophraste (vers 372 - 288 av. J.-C.) est un philosophe grec, né à Érésos (Lesbos). Disciple d’Aristote, il lui succède en 322 av. J.-C. à la tête du Lycée d’Athènes, qu’il dirige jusqu’à sa mort. Il est surtout connu pour être le père de la botanique grâce à ses traités Recherches sur les plantes et Des causes des phénomènes végétaux. Il s’intéresse aussi à la minéralogie, la zoologie, la météorologie et à l’éthique. Parmi ses écrits, les plus célèbres sont Les Caractères, portraits de types humains qui influenceront la littérature morale européenne et notamment La Bruyère. Bibliographie : - Helmut Seling, Die Augsburger Gold- und Silberschmiede 1529-1868 : Meister, Marken, Werke, Munich, 2007.

MANNER OF RICHARD LINDNER (GERMAN/AMERICAN 1901-1978) WOMAN IN PROFILE WITH RED COIFFURE oil on board; 31.5 x 25cm; 12 1/2 x 9 3/4in; (48 x 41.5cm; 18 3/4 x 16 1/4in framed) Provenance: Bill Hopkins, London

HELEN KAPP (BRITISH 1901-1978) THE FIG titled and inscribed The Fig. 3 / 31 / 37 wide CU lower centre; pen and brown ink on paper; 28 x 34cm; 11 x 13 1/4in (41 x 46cm; 16 x 18 1/4in framed). Provenance: Gillian Drey Gallery. Painter, watercolourist, illustrator and wood-engraver, Kapp grew up within a cultured émigré family in Hampstead and attended the Slade School of Art and the Central School of Art and Design. Later in life she became an influential arts administor, being appointed director of the Wakefield Art Gallery in 1951, and then the first director of the Abbot Hall Art Gallery in Kendal. Her elder brother was the portrait painter, draughtsman and caricaturist Edmond Xavier Kapp (1890-1978).

JAMES COWIE (SCOTTISH 1886-1956) VISITORS AT THE SUMMER SCHOOL OF PAINTING. pencil on paper; 12.5 x 14.5cm; 5 x 5 3/4in (32 x 33cm; 12 1/2 x 13in framed). Exhibited: London, Bourne Fine Art, 1987. The present drawing may well date from Cowie's time at the Patrick Allan School of Art at Hospitalfield, Arbroath. He moved to be warden there in 1937, ran the annual summer school and completed some of his best work, in particular portraits and group portraits of other artists and students. The latter included Robert Colquhoun, Robert MacBryde, Waistel Cooper and Joan Eardley. The son of a farmer, Cowie trained at the Glasgow School of Art. During the First World War he registered as a conscientious objector and served in the Non-Combatant Corp. After the War he taught for a number of years at Belshill Academy near Glasgow, and then as Head of Painting at Gray’s School of Art, Aberdeen before moving to Hospitafield.

LANDSCAPE WITH FIGURE LANDSCAPE WITH FIGURE dated 3.1.45 lower right; pencil and black chalk on paper; 20 x 32cm; 8 x 12 1/2in (33.5 x 45cm; 13 1/4 x 17 3/4in framed) Provenance: William Murray (from whom purchased) The present work, a page from a sketchbook, bears the considerable influence of Craxton’s greatest inspirations, including Pablo Picasso, Joan Miro, Graham Sutherland and Samuel Palmer. The subject and composition is typical of John Craxton’s work from the mid-1940s. Both surreal and Neo-romantic it belongs to a series of paintings, prints and drawings he produced focused on solitary figures. Later in life he would describe them as, ‘my means of escape and a sort of self protection. A shepherd is a lone figure, and so is a poet.’ Hemmed in by the structures of British society and the realities of war Craxton was, for a great many years, a man unsure of his place in the world. He drew the present landscape at the same time that he was working on the illustrations for A Poet's Eye, an anthology of poetry selected by Geoffrey Grigson (see lot 286). Both the poetry and the art which decorates the book powerfully speaks to the escapist mood of wartime Britain. The somewhat menacing landscape of the work also finds inspiration from the time Craxton spent painting in Pembrokeshire, alongside his mentor, Graham Sutherland.

ARNOLD NECHANSKY (AUSTRIAN 1888-1938) DESIGN FOR ENAMEL OF A SEATED NUDE watercolour over pencil on paper; 6.5cm; 2 1/2in (image diameter) (32 x 25cm; 12 1/2 x 10in framed) Provenance: Fischer Fine Art Ltd, London (from whom purchased) Nechansky studied under Oskar Strnad, Adolf Boehm and Josef Hoffmann at the Vienna School of Applied Arts (1909-1913). From 1912 he worked regularly for the Wiener Werkstätte designing postcards, jewellery, wallpaper, textiles, ceramics and silverware, and also for the J & L Lobmeyr who specialised in glassware in Vienna. In 1914 he designed the Austrian pavilion for the Cologne Werkbund exhibition, and in 1919 he was appointed to the Berlin-Charlottenburg School of Applied Arts and Crafts, where he headed the class for metal and leather work and the preparatory class for general design. In 1923 he married the painter Marianne von Winter (1902–1985) and returned to Austria in 1934. His work is notable for its combination of vibrant colours and a sense of nostalgia as in the present example (see also the following lot).

ARNOLD NECHANSKY (AUSTRIAN 1888-1938) DESIGN FOR SILVER WORK WITH HEAD OF A WOMAN. pen and black ink heightened with white gouache over traces of pencil; 8 x 8.5cm; 3 1/4 x 3 1/2in (31.5 x 25.5cm; 12 1/4 x 10in framed). Provenance: Fischer Fine Art Ltd, London (from whom purchased). see note to lot 15.

EDWARD MCKNIGHT KAUFFER (AMERICAN/BRITISH 1891-1954) TREES signed and dated E.McKnight Kauffer 1918 lower left; watercolour over traces of pencil on paper; 22.5 x 20cm; 8 3/4 x 8in (39 x 34.5cm; 15 1/4 x 13 1/2in framed) Provenance: Abbott & Holder, London Literature: Mark Hamworth-Borth, E. McKnight Kauffer, a Designer and his Public, London, 1979, p. 28 In the present work McKnight Kauffer's admiration for Cezanne and Cubism is tangible, anticipating his later bolder style and use of bright colours (see lot 160). Before leaving the USA for Europe McKnight Kauffer had visited the Armory Show of 1913 in Chicago, which introduced European avant-garde to an American audience. The work of Duchamp, Matisse, Braque, Brâncusi and Van Gogh thrilled him. He recalled: 'I didn’t understand it, but I certainly couldn’t dismiss it'. Thereafter McKnight Kauffer travelled to Munich and Paris before arriving in London at the outbreak of the First World War. Settling in Hampstead he befriended the painters Robert Bevan (lot 70), Charles Ginner and Harold Gilman, all members of the London Group, a pivotal forum for new painting and sculpture. From 1915 McKnight Kauffer worked in advertising, collaborating with Frank Pick, publicity manager of the London Underground, designing posters of sparkling originality for the tube for the next twenty-five years. His partnership with Pick established his reputation as one of the twentieth century's stand-out figures in poster and graphic design. Born Edward Kauffer in Montana, he had left school early to work as a theatre set painter before studying art in San Francisco where he subsidised his income working in a bookshop. There in 1912 he met university professor Joseph McKnight who was so impressed by Kauffer's paintings that he offered to sponsor his study trip to Europe. Kauffer added McKnight to his name as a mark of gratitude.

STUDY OF TULIPS IN A VASE Signed and dated 'Colquhoun / 37' lower right, pencil and watercolour on paper, 34 x 24cm; 13 1/4 x 9 1/2in (51 x 40cm; 20 x 15 3/4in framed). With the recent 2025 retrospective on Colquhoun at Tate Britain there has been a timely renewal of interest in the artist's extraordinary body of work and writing. Following her studies at the Slade, Colquhoun travelled to Paris where in 1931 she enrolled at the Académie Colorassi and met Surrealists Salvador Dali and André Breton. Her return to London coincided with the 1936 International Exhibition of Surrealism organised by Roland Penrose, and her solo exhibition 'Exotic Plant Decorations' at the Fine Art Society the same year focused on her botanical paintings clearly reflected the influence of Surrealism. The present work dates from these early years when she painted plants and flowers in exaggerated size and detail and explored their organic forms.

THE TEA PARTY THE TEA PARTY signed Frances Hodgkins lower right; pencil and watercolour on paper; 34 x 37cm; 13 1/2 x 14 1/2in (56 x 59.5cm; 22 x 23 1/2in framed) Exhibited: London, Gillian Jason Gallery, A Tribute to Frances Hodgkins, 1987, no. 6 Executed circa 1910, the setting of the present early watercolour suggests the south of France and, in the vibrant palette and fluid brushstrokes, the influence of Henri Matisse and Raoul Dufy. Hodgkin invariably preferred painting in watercolour and the white surface of the paper adds a sense of freedom and luminosity to the elegant gathering of ladies amidst whom a child is incongruously enjoying a swing, a somewhat arbitrary juxtaposition of compositional elements typical of the artist. Hodgkins first left her home town of Dunedin in New Zealand for Europe in 1901, moving between England and France and visiting Spain, Moroco, Italy and the Netherlands. She exhibited at the Royal Academy in London becoming the first New Zealander to have her work shown 'on the line'. In Paris she taught at the Academie Colarossi, the first woman appointed to a teaching position there, and founded a school for watercolours in Concarneau, Brittany. With the outbreak of the First World War she moved to St Ives in Cornwall where she worked with Cedric Morris and others in his circle of friends including John Piper. After the Armistice Hodgkins began showing with the London Group and in 1929 joined the Seven and Five Society, becoming a key figure in the development of British Modernism between the Wars.

CONTINENTAL SCHOOL (19TH CENTURY) AD ASTRA (TO THE STARS) signed with the artist's monogram and dated 1897 lower right; oil on canvas; 44 x 27cm; 17 1/4 x 10 3/4in (59.5 x 41.5cm; 23 1/2 x 16 1/4in framed) This enigmatic painting centred on a naked girl standing in the sea with hair on end and hands outstretched, clearly derives from Ad Astra painted in 1894 by Akseli Gallen-Kallela (Finnish 1865-1931). In the present riff on Gallen-Kallela's composition the figure stands higher in the water, the sky is cloudy, and the artist has added the heads of a man and woman floating half submerged in the water, suggesting a more sombre message. Gallen Kallela exhibited Ad Astra in Dresden and Gothenburg in 1895, thus whoever painted the present work may have seen Gallen-Kallela's painting in one of these locations, suggesting the artist could indeed be Nordic or possibly German. Gallen-Kallela painted two versions of Ad Astra, one of which is in the Gyllenberg Museum, Helsinki, and the other in the Art Institute of Chicago. The present variation appears to be in its original Art Nouveau style frame. JRT notes: Mystery Picture, signed with an indecipherable monogram and dated 1897. It is clearly some sort of parody of Gallen-Kallela's Ad Astra and I suspect Finnish or at least Scandinavian.

ROBERT COLQUHOUN (BRITISH 1914-1962) LOBSTERS signed R. Colquhoun upper right; oil on panel; 24 x 33.5cm; 9 1/2 x 13 1/4in (34 x 44cm; 13 x 17 1/4in framed) Provenance: Abbott & Holder, London Colquhoun studied at the Glasgow School of Art (1933-38) where he met Robert MacBryde, his life partner to be. Invalided out of the War in 1941 he moved to London, where he gained the support of the collector Peter Watson and worked as an ambulance driver. Working alongside MacBryde and with a studio in the same building as Jankel Adler and John Minton, his work was included in Six Scottish Painters at the Lefevre Gallery, followed by further shows at the gallery in 1944 and 1946. He also started teaching and exhibiting at the newly established Anglo-French Art School in St Johns Wood.

ITHELL COLQUHOUN (BRITISH 1906-1988) DROWNED SAILOR signed and dated Colquhoun / 36 lower right pen and brown ink and brown wash on paper 35 x 44cm; 13 3/4 x 17 1/4in 56 x 63cm; 22 x 24 3/4in (framed) see note to lot 18

MERVYN PEAKE (BRITISH 1911-1968) SKETCH OF A MAN CREATURE black chalk and ink, brown wash on paper; 20 x 28cm; 8 X 11in (36 x 43.5cm; 14 1/4 x 17in framed) This work on paper may be a sketch for one of Peake's book illustrations see note to lot 24

SLEEPING BOY SLEEPING BOY pencil on paper laid down; 28 x 20cm; 11 x 8in (49.5 x 40.5cm; 19 1/2 x 16in framed) Exhibited: London, Christopher Hull Gallery, Freud and Craxton, 1983, no. 74 Executed in 1942. There appears to be a sketch of two birds with long necks, possibly swans, on the verso, just visible through the paper. On a label on the reverse JRT notes his conversation with Craxton at the time of the 1983 exhibition: 'this drawing comes from a sketchbook which was used turn-and turn-about by John Craxton and Lucien Freud in 1942, when they were sharing a studio and socially inseperable. In the 1983 Christopher Hull show it was classified as a Craxton, but Craxton himself told me that he really could not remember for sure and thought it was more likely to be by Freud - something I think one might guess anyway from comparing it with the known contemporary works of the two artists'. Catherine Lampert, however, disputes this interpretation of the drawing considering it technically and stylisticaly to be by Craxton alone, a view also shared by Ian Collins, author of John Craxton, A Life of Gifts.

MERVYN PEAKE (BRITISH 1911-1968) TITUS ON THE CLIFF gouache on card; 23 x 33cm; 9 x 13in (44 x 54cm; 17 1/4 x 21 1/4in framed) A celebrated writer of fantasy novels and a painter-illustrator, Peake's trilogy Titus Groan(1946), Gormenghast (1950), and Titus Alone (1959) were all illustrated by him. This striking work has an unsettling angle and a claustrophobic perspective in which the hero, possibly Titus, climbs the cliff of his castle. The cliffs are reminiscent of the Chinese landscape in which Peake grew up and which is often included in his fantastic imagery. From his early twenties onwards Peake produced illustrations for a range of magazines and publications, the decade following the end of the Second World War being his greatest period of output. Living on Sark in the Channel Islands from 1946 until 1949 he produced a stream of illustrations to many of the classics, including Treasure Island, The Hunting of the Snark, The Ancient Mariner, Grimm's Household Tales (see lot 314), Alice's Adventures in Wonderland and Through the Looking Glass. He was married to the painter Maeve Gilmore (1917-1983).

CLAUD LOVAT FRASER (BRITISH 1890-1921) A FARM IN NO MAN'S LAND signed C. Lovat Fraser upper right; titled and dated Nov: 1915. Ploegsteert upper left; signed and dated C Lovat Fraser 1916 on the mount lower right; pen and black ink and blue wash on paper; 11 x 15cm; 4 1/4 x 6in (29 x 29cm; 11 1/2 x 11 1/2in framed) Lovat drew the present work at Ploegsteert in Wallonia in the autumn of 1915 following front line action in the the battle of Loos that raged between 25th September and 8th October 1915. The battle was the major Franco-British engagement with German forces that year and the first time British forces had used gas, but it ended with no consequential gains and considerable loss of life. Lovat Fraser served with the 14th Battalion of the Durham Light Infantry, but after shell shock and a further gas attack that December he was invalided back to England in early 1916. After the War he collaborated with the Curwen Press on paper patterns (see lot 270), book illustrations, book plates and advertisements, and produced set and costume designs for the theatre and ballet including for productions at Stratford-on-Avon, the Lyric Hammersmith and the Coliseum. He married the American actress Grace Inez Cranford in 1917 with whom he had a daughter. He died suddenly on a family holiday in Dymchurch, Kent.

HAROLD YATES (BRITISH 1916-2001) SHEPHERD'S HUT BELOW WINTER TREES. signed and dated Yates 1940 lower right; pen and ink and watercolour; 36 x 24cm; 14 1/4 x 9 1/2in (54 x 40.5cm; 21 1/4 x 16in framed). Exhibited: London, The Belgrave Gallery, Harold Yates, London, 1989 (from whom purchased). The son of a cartoonist, Yates studied at Portsmouth School of Art and worked as a commercial artist before having his first solo exhibition at Foyles Gallery. During the Second World War he served in the 5th Batallion of the Duke of Cornwall's Light Infantry and completed a number of watercolours recording everyday life in the ranks, some bought by the War Artist's Advisory Committee and now in the Imperial War Museum.

EDMUND DULAC (FRENCH-BRITISH 1882-1953) DESIGN FOR A RUG pencil and gouache on paper; 12.5 x 9cm; 5 x 3 1/2in (26 x 21cm; 10 1/4 x 8 1/4in framed) Provenance: Hartnoll & Eyre, London (purchased in 1970) Exhibited: London, Hartnoll & Eyre, Edmund Dulac 1882-1953, 1970, no. 34 see note to lot 62

AFTER MADEMOISELLE LOIR, ENGRAVED BY TARDIEU LE FILS - Lot 1 AFTER MADEMOISELLE LOIR, ENGRAVED BY TARDIEU LE FILS "Portrait of Madame du Boccage (1710-1802) surmounting one of her poems". "Around this Portrait crowned by glory, I see the loves fluttering: And the temple of Gnide and that of memory Will always vie for it." Framed burin engraving. H. 15 x W. 9 cm. (Minor foxing at lower right). Note: Madame du Boccage (née Marie-Anne de Roumier) was a French writer and poetess of the 18th century. Known for her literary talents and cultivated mind, she distinguished herself in the literary salons of her day, notably through her poems and philosophical writings. She was one of the first women to be involved in epic poetry, and her work, marked by a strong sensibility, explored themes such as love, nature and the female condition.

ENGRAVED BY GIUSEPPE VASI (1710-1782) - Lot 2 ENGRAVED BY GIUSEPPE VASI (1710-1782) "View of the Castel Sant'Angelo in Rome from the Porta Castello". Framed antique engraving. Period: 18th century. Sight size: H. 19 x W. 29 cm. Note: Giuseppe Vasi (1710-1782) was an 18th-century Italian artist and engraver, renowned for his views and panoramas of Rome. Vasi stands out for his exceptional talent in capturing the cityscapes and monuments of the Eternal City, at a time when Rome was a center of artistic and cultural activity. His engravings are a veritable source of visual documentation of 18th-century Rome, often produced in etching and published in works such as "Vedute di Roma" (Views of Rome), of which our engraving is a part (1746). Through his work, he succeeded in blending architectural precision with artistic sensitivity, offering his contemporaries and future generations a window on the city's past. His works influenced not only the art of engraving, but also the iconography of Baroque Rome, helping to spread its image throughout Europe.

Frans Pourbus the Elder (1545-1581), entourage of - Lot 7 Frans Pourbus the Elder (1545-1581), entourage of Portrait of a couple and their children, circa 1580 Oil on panel, pair. H_ 36 cm W_ 28 cm Coat of arms added later. Cracks, restorations, oxidized varnish. Interesting late XIXᵉ restorer's labels on verso. Provenance: - Pérusse des Cars family - Duc des Cars Presented as a diptych, this painting depicts the commissioner and his wife kneeling in landscapes. They are accompanied by their children. The red crosses above some of the children's heads indicate that they are deceased. Our painting may represent François de Pérusse des Cars, accompanied by his first wife, Claudine de Baufremont, who died prematurely, and his second wife, Isabeau de Beauville. The lord is wearing the grand collar of the Order of the Holy Spirit, created by Henri III in 1578, of which our model was one of the first recipients. This element, combined with his remarriage in 1579, allows us to date the painting after this date. We can also note that the coats of arms were added later, probably in the XIXᵉ century. It was indeed common practice to embellish old paintings with coats of arms or the names of the models in order to facilitate the reading of ancestor galleries. Expert : Cabinet ARTÉON

French school of the late 18th or early 19th century - Lot 15 French school of the late 18th or early 19th century Head of Heliodorus driven from the temple after Raphael Oil on canvas. H_38 cm W_46 cm Marked "Tête du naufrage de la méduse, étude, attributed to Géricault" by another hand, "du Duc de Vitry". Our intriguing painting takes the head of Heliodorus from the composition painted by Raphael between 1511 and 1512 in the Vatican Stanze. Heliodorus of Antioch, then minister to the Syrian king Seleucus IV, was in charge of desecrating and pillaging the Temple of Jerusalem. The scene shows the desecrator, on the right-hand edge of the composition, being thrown to the ground by a divine rider in response to the prayer of the priest Aria, who occupies the center of the composition. This powerful subject, depicting a man thrown at the feet of a horse, deeply fascinated painters, who regularly reworked it. A number of copies have been made, focusing on the face of Heliodorus. In particular, an engraving preserved in the collection assembled by Ingres and now housed at the Musée Ingres Bourdelle (box no. 3 in the studio documentation assembled by the painter). Created with both lightness and strength, our Héliodore is treated in the manner of a sketch and was probably executed in front of the fresco. It seems essential to relate our painting to the drawing made in Rome by Jean-Pierre Saint-Ours (1752-1809) and kept at the Musée d'art et d'histoire de Genève. This drawing testifies to the painter's interest in this Raphael-inspired face. It also highlights the perspective work required to respect the figure's exaggerated, Mannerist character, while softening its features and isolating it in a tighter frame. In his painting The Earthquake (1792-1799), he used a similar perspective for the central figure. Raphael's work in the Stanze, the rooms adjoining the Sistine Chapel, was a real inspiration for painters working in Rome. The young Géricault's body of work includes several sketches inspired by the figure of Heliodorus. This study has long been attributed to him, as evidenced by an old inscription on the stretcher. Expert : Cabinet ARTÉON

Ambroise DUBOIS (1543-1614) - Lot 20 Ambroise DUBOIS (1543-1614) Venus and Love, Before 1595 Oil on canvas H_141 cm W_90 cm (Faded, edges repaired and restored, in a XIXᵉ frame). In front of her unmade bed, Love at her side, Venus is depicted full-length, her quiver on the ground and an arrow in her hand. In this classic Renaissance depiction, Dubois uses the colors of his Italian inspiration, blended with the serpentine figure of the Mannerist. serpentine figure of the Antwerp mannerism of his training. A native of Flanders, we know little about the early life of this major artist of the Second Fontainebleau School. Dubois concentrated mainly on painting large-scale decors, notably in Fontainebleau, where from 1595 he worked on embellishing the château for Henri IV and Marie de Médicis. Our painting, whose commission remains a mystery, may have been part of a larger cycle. However, a monogram present in various places in the painting, such as on the bed and the quiver, could give us a clue as to who commissioned it, a person who remains unidentified today. Our painting, which we fully attribute to the artist, suffers today from restorations that hamper the reading of the work. In fact, some of the work's particularly covering interventions bear witness more to the restorer's heavy-handedness than to the painter's. than that of the painter. This attribution is also taken up by the Revue des musées de France in its recent publication of our work. Bibliography - Nouveaux éclairages sur Giampietrino, Jacob de Backer and Antoine Caron, Revue des musées de France. Revue du Louvre, 3, 2025, fig. 11 p. 54. Expert : Cabinet ARTÉON

Cornelis DE VOS (1585-1651), follower of - Lot 21 Cornelis DE VOS (1585-1651), follower of The Triumph of Bacchus Oil on canvas. H_162 cm L_233 cm (Faded). Invented by Rubens (1577-1640) for the decorations of Philip IV of Spain's hunting lodge, Torre de la Parada, the composition was executed by an influential member of his entourage, Cornelis de Vos. Well-versed in his master's technique, but with his own interpretation, de Vos produced a true masterpiece, one of the most ambitious cycles undertaken by Rubens' workshop. Our painting, derived directly from Cornelis de Vos' work now in the Prado Museum, is a true testament to the taste for works from Rubens' workshop. With its large dimensions, our canvas is one of the few quality reproductions of this subject. Another canvas of a similar format was in the collection of the Duke of Buccleuch of Queensberry. Expert : Cabinet ARTÉON

Sir Tony Cragg CBE RA, British b.1949 - Hollow Columns, 2006; bronze on steel base, unique, from a series of 7 different versions, incised with artist’s signature and stamped with foundry mark 'Schmäke Düsseldorf' on base, H109 x W55 x D40.2 cm including base (taller figure H107 cm and smaller figure H103 cm) (ARR) Sir Tony Cragg CBE RA, British b.1949 - Hollow Columns, 2006; bronze on steel base, unique, from a series of 7 different versions, incised with artist’s signature and stamped with foundry mark 'Schmäke Düsseldorf' on base, H109 x W55 x D40.2 cm including base (taller figure H107 cm and smaller figure H103 cm) (ARR). Note: Cragg has always been interested in the idea of creating new forms, over representing terrestrial objects. In the present lot, the sculpture appears in motion, flowing through space despite its physical heaviness of bronze. His practice is guided by the 'intelligence of materials', whereby the material has intrinsic prosperities that guide the sculptural process of the artist. He has created a new visual language that has cemented him as one the most influential sculptors of his generation. In 1988, he won both the Turner Prize and was selected to represent the UK at the Venice Biennale. Through various teaching positions as Professor at Ecole Nationale Superieure des Beaux Arts, Paris, France (1999-2009) and Professor at Kunstakademie, Dusseldorf, Germany (2009-2013) he has influenced future artists. His works are in major collections including the Tate London, MoMA, New York, the Centre Pompidou, Paris. In 2008 he opened Waldfrieden Sculpture Park in Germany, a permanent space for exhibition and his own practice.

Barbara Kruger, American b.1945 - Untitled (Love), 2001; chromogenic dye coupler print in artist's frame, from an edition of 10, 215 x 125 cm Barbara Kruger, American b.1945 - Untitled (Love), 2001; chromogenic dye coupler print in artist's frame, from an edition of 10, 215 x 125 cm. Provenance: with Sprüth Magers Lee, London (according to the label attached to the reverse). Note: using her signature powerful fonts and texts overlaid onto a black and white image, this series from 2001 includes works titled 'Lust, Evil, Fate, Help, Envy, Hate, Fear, Pray, Good, and Love', to encapsulate and provoke essential human emotions. By pairing the same image with strongly opposing emotions, it prompts the viewer to examine how experience these emotions both internally and in society. She was recently the subject of a solo exhibition at The Serpentine, London in early 2024. In 2021, Kruger was included in Time magazine's list of the 100 Most Influential People. Solo exhibitions have been held worldwide since the 1970s including The Museum of Contemporary Art, Los Angeles, the Whitney Museum of American Art, New York, Moderna Museet, Stockholm, Pinakothek der Moderne, Munich; National Gallery of Art, Washington, DC; Amorepacific Museum of Art, Seoul, the Art Institute of Chicago, and the Los Angeles County Museum of Art.

Sandra Blow RA, British 1925-2006 - Reflection (Sand Ridges: Egyptian Connotations); oil and mixed media on canvas, 121.7 x 121.7 cm (ARR) Sandra Blow RA, British 1925-2006 - Reflection (Sand Ridges: Egyptian Connotations); oil and mixed media on canvas, 121.7 x 121.7 cm (ARR) Provenance: with Lemon Street Gallery, Truro, 2001 (by repute); Bonhams, London, 19th November 2013, Modern British, Irish & East Anglian Art, lot 176; Private Collection, purchased from the above Note: Blow is considered to be one of the pioneering abstract artists of the 1950s who introduced a new form of expressive painting to Britain, often using everyday discarded materials such as sawdust, sackcloth and plaster. This practice was developed in tandem with her then-partner, the great Italian artist, Alberto Burri, with whom she travelled and worked in Italy during the late 1940s. Although considerably younger than many of her contemporaries of this early period, she was at the forefront of the abstract movement in Britain. The present work is an brilliant example of her later period, following her move to St. Ives in 1994, and shows her brilliant synthesis of form, light, space, texture and rhythm.

Barbara Kruger, American b.1945 - Untitled (Hate), 2001; chromogenic dye coupler print in artist's frame, from an edition of 10, 215 x 125 cm Barbara Kruger, American b.1945 - Untitled (Hate), 2001; chromogenic dye coupler print in artist's frame, from an edition of 10, 215 x 125 cm. Provenance: with Sprüth Magers Lee, London (according to the label attached to the reverse). Note: using her signature powerful fonts and texts overlaid onto a black and white image, this series from 2001 includes works titled 'Lust, Evil, Fate, Help, Envy, Hate, Fear, Pray, Good, and Love', to encapsulate and provoke essential human emotions. By pairing the same image with strongly opposing emotions, it prompts the viewer to examine how experience these emotions both internally and in society. She was recently the subject of a solo exhibition at The Serpentine, London in early 2024. In 2021, Kruger was included in Time magazine's list of the 100 Most Influential People. Solo exhibitions have been held worldwide since the 1970s including The Museum of Contemporary Art, Los Angeles, the Whitney Museum of American Art, New York, Moderna Museet, Stockholm, Pinakothek der Moderne, Munich; National Gallery of Art, Washington, DC; Amorepacific Museum of Art, Seoul, the Art Institute of Chicago, and the Los Angeles County Museum of Art.

Joseph Biel was born in Grodno, Poland (later considered part of Russia). He studied at the Russian Academy in Paris, and at the Workman's College in Melbourne, Australia. While living in Australia he established the first Jewish library in Melbourne. Upon his arrival in the United States, he studied with George Grosz at the Art Students League.

A VIP tour of the magnificent Newby Hall and Gardens near Ripon for up to 20 people between April and the end of September 2026, offering a unique opportunity to explore the house before it is open to the public, in the company of an experienced guide. You'll be welcomed in the entrance hall, served coffee and biscuits, and presented with a complimentary copy of the house guidebook. You will then receive a conducted tour of the house, which will provide a special insight into its history, contents and ownership. There’s been an estate at Newby, near Harrogate in North Yorkshire, since the Middle Ages, although parts of the current house date from the late 1600s. It was in the 18th century that Newby Hall took on the character we see today, and it’s become renowned as an exceptional example of Georgian interior decoration and grand tour acquisitions. Roman Statuary, Gobelin tapestries and suites of Chippendale furniture are positioned as the then owner, William Wedell prescribed in the Robert Adam designed rooms

David Roberts RA (1796-1864) Five lithographic prints of eastern subjects: 'Mosque of the Sultan Hassan from the Great Square of The Rumeyleh'; 'Interior of the Mosque of the Sultan Hassan'; 'Shrine of The Holy Sepulchre'; 'Chancel of the Church of St Helena'; and another mosque interior. Signed in the plate, published by Day & Son, 1855/56. Condition Report: Three with minor foxing, some minor discolouration. Sold for £230.

A champagne afternoon tea for two in a central London Georgian townhouse whose authentic restoration won a Georgian Group Architectural Award. The winners will be given a guided tour of the house by its owner and learn about the house's connection to Napoleon Bonaparte, before sitting down in the candlelit dining room with its 1790s wallpaper (see picture) to enjoy a formally waitered, luxury afternoon tea. Redeemable within 12 months.

A set of oak filing drawers early 20th century, the tambour front enclosing twenty short drawers, 40cm wide, 50cm deep, 133cm high. Minor wear to top left corner and one jamb, tambour rises and fall but not below the third drawer from the base.

A folio of thirty-three architectural engravings 18th century, including: 'South Front of Kedleston' 26 x 64cm; 'The Elevation of Castle Howard, towards the gardens, in Yorkshire' 25 x 64cm; 'The East Prospect of St Phillip's Church in Birmingham' 38 x 28cm; 'The Gardens and Plantations, Clare Mont in Surrey' 37 x 49cm. (33) Condition Report: many with joins and folds, some discolouration, largely good. Sold for £380.

Thomas Malton the Younger (1748-1804) 'Melbourne House, White-Hall' hand-coloured etching and aquatint, published Jan 18th 1797 by T Malton. 25 x 31cm. The house was built 1755-58 by James Paine for Sir Matthew Fetherstonhaugh, enlarged from 1787 as York House for Frederick, Duke of York, by architect Henry Holland. The print shows Holland's portico. From 1793 to 1830, the house was owned by the Melbourne family, thereafter by Baron Dover until 1885. It then became the Scottish Office and now Scotland Office. Condition Report: Good, modern frame.

Alma Singer (British 1986-), 'This Too Shall Pass (2)', 2025 Alma Singer (British 1986-), 'This Too Shall Pass (2)', 2025, unique, crayon on paper, signed and dated in pencil, published by Jealous Gallery; sheet: 40 x 30cm. Alma Singer is the professional alias adopted by the talented artist Carla Nizzola, who resides in the picturesque county of Kent. Carla's artistic creations are distinguished by their unique blend of playfulness and depth, inviting viewers to engage with a range of complex themes. Her work often explores the intricate concepts of value—both in terms of material worth and the intrinsic value of artistic expression. Through her art, she challenges conventional notions of identity, delving into the multifaceted nature of self-perception and societal expectations. One of the central themes in Carla's work is female sexuality, which she approaches with a refreshing candor and nuance. By addressing this often-taboo subject, she encourages open dialogue and reflection, empowering her audience to confront their own beliefs and biases. Her art serves as a mirror, reflecting the diverse experiences of womanhood and the myriad ways in which female identity can be expressed and understood.

JEAN GAUMY 1948- - Lot 1 JEAN GAUMY 1948- "Pleine Mer", Éditions de la Martinière, Paris, 2001, 288 pages. Book. Original French edition; illustrated publisher's boards. For fourteen years, Magnum photographer Jean Gaumy shared the day-to-day life of fishermen aboard ocean-going trawlers. Extracts from his notebooks and over 120 photographs, many of them reproduced on double-page spreads, tell the story of this period. In-4 (29.5 x 25 cm). Ref: M.Parr & G.Badger, The Photobook: A History, vol II, Phaidon, Paris, 2007, p.315. Very good condition; bookseller's price label still affixed to 4th cover.

CLAUDE RAYMOND-DITYVON 1937-2008 - Lot 2 CLAUDE RAYMOND-DITYVON 1937-2008 "Marée noire 78", co-published by Viva/Maison de la Culture de Rennes, 1978, 64 pages. Paperback. Rare first edition of this pamphlet "Black tide resulting from the greedy blackness of a few for whom enrichment is all that counts...". Bilingual text (English-French) by Georges-Emmanuel Clancier; poem by Youenn Gwernig. Catalog of the exhibition at the Maison de la Culture de Rennes. In-8 oblong (13 x 18 cm) Freckles on title page, otherwise fair condition.

JOAN FONTCUBERTA 1955- [signed] - Lot 3 JOAN FONTCUBERTA 1955- [signed] "Le baiser de Judas - Photographie et vérité", Actes Sud, Arles, 1996, 128 pages. Signed by Joan Fontcuberta on the title page. Original paperback edition; illustrated softcover. Text translated from Spanish by Claude Bleton. The camera doesn't lie, every photograph is an evidence; behind this reassuring belief lies a betrayal; Joan Fontcuberta in a series of essays illustrated with a few photographs tries to disentangle truth from falsehood and reality from fiction. In-8 (19 x 10 cm) Very good condition.

RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 - Lot 4 RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 "Les sixties", Éditions Plume, 1999, 238 pages. Book. Original French edition, bound in illustrated dust jacket. Text by Doon Arbus. Photographs by Richard Avedon. Doon Arbus and Richard Avedon collaborated for thirty years on this book. Through their photographs and interviews, the spirit of an era that has remained intact, as if captured, shines through. In-4 (33 x 26 cm) Good condition; tiny tear to cover.

RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 - Lot 5 RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 "An autobiography", Schirmer/Mosel, 1993, 432 pages. Book; brown cloth binding with title in red on first board and photographic label (portrait of Avedon) on fourth board. Original English edition, with French text inserted on loose leaf; 280 photographs printed in duotone. Large in-4 (36.5 x 28.5 cm) A fine copy.

RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 - Lot 6 RICHARD AVEDON 1923-2004 "Photographies 1947-1977", Denoël-Filipacchi, 1978, 162 photographs. Book. Original French edition; layout by Elisabeth Paul. Illustrated boards. In-4 (36 x 27 cm) Good condition.

IRVING PENN 1917-2009 - Lot 7 IRVING PENN 1917-2009 "Le Bain - Dancers' Workshop of San Francisco", Maison Européenne de la Photographie, Paris, 1997, 48 pages. Hardcover with illustrated dust jacket. Original French edition; introductory text titled "du pouvoir d'étonner" by Edmonde Charles-Roux; bilingual text (English-French). 14 photographs by Irving Penn. In-4 (28 x 24 cm) Good condition.

GUY LE QUERREC 1941- [signed and dedicated] - Lot 8 GUY LE QUERREC 1941- [signed and dedicated] "Quelque part", Éditions Contrejour, Paris, 1977, 72 pages. Dedicated and signed by Guy le Querrec. Original paperback edition; some sixty photographs reproduced in black and white; the photographer's first book. In-8 (24 x 20.5 cm) Good condition, despite slightly rubbed covers.

WILLY RONIS 1910-2009 [signed] - Lot 9 WILLY RONIS 1910-2009 [signed] "Derrière l'objectif de Willy Ronis - photos et propos", Hoëbeke, Paris, 2001, 160 pages. Book, signed on title page by Willy Ronis. Original paperback edition; titled softcover. Willy Ronis selected 120 photographs for this book. Famous or lesser-known, they are all representative of his career in five key words: Patience, Reflection, Chance, Form and Time. In-8 (25 x 19.5 cm) Handling wear.

WILLY RONIS 1910-2009 [signed] - Lot 10 WILLY RONIS 1910-2009 [signed] "Sur le fil du hasard", Contrejour, Paris, 1986, 160 pages. Signed on the title page by Willy Ronis. Second edition, bound in an illustrated dust jacket. This beautifully printed second edition differs from the original in that it has a dust jacket with bilingual English/French text. A monograph on the work of Willy Ronis. The original edition of this book won the Prix Nadar in 1981 for the best photography book of the year. In-4 (30.5 x 24 cm). Dust jacket with wear.

DEBBIE FLEMING CAFFERY (1948) [dedicated and signed] - Lot 11 DEBBIE FLEMING CAFFERY (1948) [dedicated and signed] "Debbie Fleming Caffery", Filigranes Éditions, collection L'oiseau rare, 2000, 64 pages. Book, dedicated and signed by Debbie Fleming Caffery on title page. Original paperback edition; illustrated cover with large flap. Preface by Alain Desvergnes. Text and interview by Gabriel Bauret. The L'oiseau rare collection highlights a significant group of images on the theme of childhood in the career of a contemporary photographer. In-8 (17 x 13 cm) Very good condition.

ANTOINE D'AGATA 1961- - Lot 12 ANTOINE D'AGATA 1961- "Position(s)", Avarie Artbooks, 2012, 124 pages. Book. Original paperback edition. Complete with large fold-out poster inset on 3rd cover. In-12 (18 x 13 cm). Very good condition.

SOPHIE RISTELHUEBER 1949- - Lot 13 SOPHIE RISTELHUEBER 1949- "WB - West Bank", Thames and Hudson, London, 2005, 108 pages. Book. Original English edition; illustrated hardback. In November 2003, and again from February to March 2004, Sophie Ristelhueber spent two periods in the West Bank, photographing the severed roads that symbolize the separation between peoples. This book brings together her images of bruised landscapes. In-12 (17 x 11 cm) Mint condition.
